rules.title: Unique requirement identifier
rules.objective: Ensure each requirement is uniquely identifiable
checks: Verify the requirement has an identifier
Verify the identifier is unique across the requirement set
Verify the identifier adheres to the project's specified formatting or naming convention

rules.title: Unambiguous requirement wording
checks: Detect ambiguous or subjective terms (e.g., fast, reliable, adequate)
Verify all terms are defined or measurable

rules.title: Operational modes specified
checks: Verify operational modes or conditions are explicitly stated
Flag missing or implicit operational assumptions
Verify behavior during mode transitions is explicitly defined

rules.title: Acceptance criteria defined
rules.objective: Ensure requirement can be accepted objectively
checks: Detect measurable criteria or referenced verification method
Flag requirements without acceptance criteria

rules.title: Safety requirements reference DAL
rules.objective: Ensure safety requirements reference failure conditions and DAL
checks: Identify safety-related requirements
Verify associated failure condition and DAL are specified

rules.title: Requirement source identified
checks: Verify presence of source or rationale
Classify source (regulatory, customer, derived)

rules.title: Derived requirements justified
checks: Identify derived requirements
Verify justification or derivation rationale exists
Verify derived requirements are routed to the safety process (FHA/PSSA) for hazard evaluation

rules.title: Coverage of system functions
rules.objective: Ensure all FHA functions are covered
checks: Compare requirement coverage against FHA functions
Identify missing functional coverage

rules.title: Interface requirements completeness
checks: Verify protocols, data formats, and timing are defined
Flag partially specified interfaces
Verify system response to invalid inputs, out-of-range data, or missing signals is defined (Robustness)

rules.title: Environmental requirements specified
rules.objective: Ensure environmental constraints are stated where applicable
checks: Detect environmental constraints applicability
Verify explicit limits are defined

rules.title: No contradictory requirements
checks: Perform pairwise semantic contradiction analysis
Flag conflicting constraints or behaviors
Flag semantic equivalence
Flag subsuming

rules.title: Consistent terminology
checks: Extract key terms
Detect inconsistent usage or definitions
Verify terms against the approved project Data Dictionary or Glossary

rules.title: Consistent units and tolerances
checks: Detect physical quantities
Verify unit and tolerance consistency

rules.title: Consistent DAL allocation
rules.objective: Ensure DAL consistency with safety assessments
checks: Compare DAL assignments with FHA/PSSA
Flag mismatches

rules.title: Performance feasibility
rules.objective: Ensure performance is consistent with constraints
checks: Compare performance requirements with HW/SW constraints
Flag infeasible requirements

rules.title: Timing consistency
rules.objective: Ensure timing constraints do not conflict
checks: Extract timing constraints
Detect conflicting latency or period requirements

rules.title: Requirement is verifiable
checks: Verify a verification approach exists
Reject unverifiable statements

rules.title: Quantified performance thresholds
checks: Detect numeric thresholds and tolerances
Flag vague performance statements

rules.title: Subjective terms supported
checks: Detect subjective wording
Verify presence of measurable criteria

rules.title: Verification method mapped
checks: Verify mapping to test, analysis, inspection, or demonstration
Verify the specific phase or level of verification is identified (e.g., equipment level, system level, flight test)

rules.title: Verification feasibility
rules.objective: Ensure verification can realistically be performed
checks: Assess feasibility against project test capabilities and constraints

rules.title: No impossible verification conditions
checks: Detect impossible states or undefined references

rules.title: Traceability to higher-level requirements
checks: Verify link to aircraft, regulatory, or customer requirement
Identify orphaned requirements (requirements with no parent trace)

rules.title: Derived requirement traceability
checks: Verify link to safety assessment or design decision

rules.title: Downward traceability
checks: Verify trace to design or code elements
Ensure 100% downward coverage (no requirements lacking downstream allocation)

rules.title: Allocation documented
checks: Verify allocation documentation exists

rules.title: FHA to safety requirement traceability
checks: Verify each FHA failure condition maps to safety requirements

rules.title: Correct system behavior
checks: Compare requirement intent with system definition
Verify adherence to known physical or mathematical boundaries

rules.title: No unnecessary design constraints
checks: Detect implementation-specific wording
Verify the requirement states 'what' the system must do, not 'how' it should be implemented

rules.title: Correct modal verbs usage
checks: Verify 'shall' used for mandatory statements
Detect improper use of 'will', 'must', or 'may' for binding requirements

rules.title: Single requirement per statement
checks: Detect multiple obligations in one statement
Identify conjunctions (and, or, but) that improperly combine multiple verifiable actions

rules.title: Appropriate abstraction level
checks: Assess abstraction relative to requirement level

rules.title: Explicit assumptions
checks: Detect implicit assumptions
Verify explicit documentation
